text:
I hired my first AI employee.
$1,200/month. 168 hours a week. Never calls in sick.

My best human hire costs 10x that — and delivers 40 hours.

That math breaks everything we thought we knew about workforce economics.

Here are 10 things that shifted how I think about the Agent Era:

1/ Digital FTEs Are Workforce — Not Tools
168 hrs/week. Zero sick days. Zero overtime. That is 4.2x the weekly output of your best human hire.

2/ $500-2K/month vs $4K-8K+
Encode your best process once into an AI agent. It runs forever, flawlessly, at a fraction of the cost.

3/ 9,000 hrs/year vs 2,000
One Digital FTE = 4.5 years of human work in a single calendar year. Startup speed without headcount.

4/ 99%+ Consistency vs 85-95%
Humans have good days and bad days. A Digital FTE delivers the same quality at 3 AM that it does at 9 AM.

5/ The 10-80-10 Rule
Humans set intent (10%) — AI executes (80%) — Humans verify (10%).
You are not being replaced. You are being promoted.

6/ Instant Cloning vs Months of Hiring
Got a workflow that works? Spin up 10 agents in minutes — not quarters.

7/ Spec-Driven Development
Write your expertise once. Deploy it as an agent forever. Your knowledge becomes infrastructure that runs while you sleep.

8/ Zero Overhead
No benefits. No HR. No sick leave. No performance reviews. Pure outcomes.

9/ SaaS to Agent Era
Software sold you seats. AI sells you outcomes.
The business model of tomorrow is already here today.

10/ Five Maturity Levels
Level 1: AI Awareness (10-20% gain).
Level 5: AI-First Enterprise (10x productivity).
Digital FTEs are the bridge that takes you up.

The companies that reach Levels 3-5 first won't just move faster.
They'll be in a completely different category.

Where is your organization on that ladder right now?

#AgentFactory #DigitalFTE #AIEmployee #FutureOfWork #AIAgents #LearnInPublic #10x #AIFirst #Automation #WorkforceDevelopment
